_meta.method: PubMed search across 3 axes — chokepoint × fungus, species × indication, multilingual catch-net (English-abstract-indexed Asian papers). Tool: mcp__plugin_pubmed_PubMed__search_articles + get_article_metadata. All citations via PubMed.
_meta.scope_note: Breadth pass: titles + abstracts only (no full-text reads). Phase 5 will deep-dive top candidates. Open-access broad reviews (e.g. NLRP3 mechanism reviews not directly fungal) were filtered out of the by_chokepoint extraction.
_meta.attribution: PubMed via NCBI E-utilities. DOIs preserved per article.

_summary.total_pubmed_results_estimated: >8000 across all axes (most off-target, primary signal concentrated in XO/NLRP3/hyperuricemia axes; URAT1/ABCG2 thinner; C5aR1 essentially zero direct fungal antagonist hits)

XO_xanthine_oxidase.title: Hypouricemic effect of 2,4-dihydroxybenzoic acid methyl ester in hyperuricemic mice through inhibiting XOD and down-regulating URAT1.
XO_xanthine_oxidase.finding: DAE (Ganoderma applanatum) reduces serum uric acid in mice from 407 to 134 µmol/L via dual XOD inhibition and URAT1 down-regulation — DUAL-CHOKEPOINT hit, in vivo, structurally allopurinol-like.

XO_xanthine_oxidase.title: Anti-gout activity and the interaction mechanisms between Sanghuangporus vaninii active components and xanthine oxidase.
XO_xanthine_oxidase.finding: Davallialactone isolated from S. vaninii at 97.7% purity exhibits mixed XO inhibition with IC50 = 90.07 µM.

XO_xanthine_oxidase.title: Inhibition of xanthine oxidase-catalyzed xanthine and 6-mercaptopurine oxidation by luteolin, naringenin, myricetin, ampelopsin and their conjugated metabolites.
XO_xanthine_oxidase.fungus: n/a (plant flavonoids — included as substrate-pharmacology reference)
XO_xanthine_oxidase.finding: Reference paper for flavonoid XO mechanism — sulfate/glucuronide conjugates often retain inhibitory activity, important for mushroom-derived flavonoid bioavailability framing.

XO_xanthine_oxidase.title: Novel xanthine oxidase-based cell model using HK-2 cell for screening antihyperuricemic functional compounds.
XO_xanthine_oxidase.finding: HK-2 cell-based XO assay — useful screening platform for fungal extracts.

XO_xanthine_oxidase.title: Diacylglycerol from camellia oil improves hyperuricemia by inhibiting xanthine oxidase and modulating gut microbiota.
XO_xanthine_oxidase.finding: Off-target for our scope but useful microbiota-axis comparator.

XO_xanthine_oxidase.title: Galangin Protects against Hyperuricemia via Ameliorating Gut-Kidney Axis Dysfunction in Mice.
XO_xanthine_oxidase.finding: Flavonoid hits XO + gut-kidney axis. Comparator for fungal flavonoid programs.

XO_xanthine_oxidase.title: Hypouricemic Effect of 2,5-Dihydroxyacetophenone, a Computational Screened Bioactive Compound from [Cordyceps], on Hyperuricemic Mice.
XO_xanthine_oxidase.finding: DHAP from Cordyceps reduces SUA, inhibits hepatic XOD, computationally screened against XO active site.

URAT1.title: Hypouricemic effect of 2,4-dihydroxybenzoic acid methyl ester in hyperuricemic mice through inhibiting XOD and down-regulating URAT1.
URAT1.finding: Same DAE entry — also down-regulates URAT1 (this is the load-bearing dual-chokepoint hit).

URAT1.title: Cordycepin, a Characteristic Bioactive Constituent in [Cordyceps], Ameliorates Hyperuricemia through URAT1 in Hyperuricemic Mice.
URAT1.finding: Cordycepin lowers SUA from 337 to 203 µmol/L via URAT1 modulation — direct URAT1 chokepoint hit, dose-response, mouse model.

URAT1.title: Sanghuangporus vaninii ethanol extract alleviates hyperuricemic renal injury by regulating the uric acid transporters and inhibiting HK-2 apoptosis.
URAT1.finding: S. vaninii ethanol extract regulates uric acid transporters (URAT1/GLUT9/OAT1/ABCG2 family) — extract-level evidence; component-level identification still needed.

URAT1.title: Biotransformation-derived metabolites from Astragalus membranaceus and Cordyceps militaris alleviate hyperuricemia via multi-target regulation.
URAT1.finding: AMC-BFE downregulates URAT1 + GLUT9, alters ABCG2, activates PPARα — multi-chokepoint extract; LC-MS/MS profile shows isoflavone aglycones, nucleosides, polyols. URAT1 + ABCG2 + PPARα triple-leverage.

URAT1.title: Caffeic acid phenethyl ester alleviated hypouricemia in hyperuricemic mice through inhibiting XOD and up-regulating OAT3.
URAT1.fungus: n/a (CAPE — propolis component, not fungal but co-indexed)
URAT1.finding: CAPE hits XOD + OAT3. Propolis-derived comparator (non-fungal).

ABCG2.title: Fuling-Zexie formula attenuates hyperuricemia-induced nephropathy and inhibits JAK2/STAT3 signaling and NLRP3 inflammasome activation in mice.
ABCG2.fungus: Poria cocos (component of FZ formula — Wolfiporia extensa, sclerotium-forming fungus)
ABCG2.compound: FZ formula (mixed); Poria cocos contribution unspecified
ABCG2.finding: FZ formula modulates ABCG2/GLUT9/OAT1 expression and suppresses NLRP3 inflammasome in HUA mice. Poria cocos is the only fungal ingredient. Hits ABCG2 + NLRP3 simultaneously — multi-chokepoint TCM formula.

ABCG2.title: Host-derived [Lactobacillus] alleviates hyperuricemia by improving gut microbial community and hydrolase-mediated degradation of purine nucleosides.
ABCG2.finding: Probiotic strain enhances ABCG2 expression in kidney + downregulates GLUT9 — relevant chokepoint biology comparator, not fungal.

ABCG2.title: Tryptophan Metabolism-Regulating Probiotics Alleviate Hyperuricemia by Protecting the Gut Barrier Integrity and Enhancing Colonic Uric Acid Excretion.
ABCG2.finding: Indole metabolites correlate with ABCG2 expression — not fungal; mechanism comparator for AhR-pathway chokepoint biology.

NLRP3_inflammasome.title: Fuling-Zexie formula attenuates hyperuricemia-induced nephropathy and inhibits JAK2/STAT3 signaling and NLRP3 inflammasome activation in mice.
NLRP3_inflammasome.finding: Suppresses NLRP3 + ASC + cleaved-caspase-1 in HUA-nephropathy mouse model.

NLRP3_inflammasome.title: Exopolysaccharides of Ganoderma lucidum Activate the NLRP3 Inflammasome, Modulating Phagocytes and the Response of Mice to Infection with Cryptococcus neoformans.
NLRP3_inflammasome.finding: DIRECTIONALITY CAVEAT: G. lucidum EPS ACTIVATE (not inhibit) NLRP3 via dectin-1/Syk/NF-κB — protective in cryptococcosis but counterproductive for gout. Critical for downstream framing — Ganoderma polysaccharides are NLRP3 activators in some contexts.

NLRP3_inflammasome.title: Sporoderm-removed ganoderma lucidum spore powder (S-GLSP) alleviates neuroinflammation injury by regulating microglial polarization through inhibition of NLRP3 inflammasome activation.
NLRP3_inflammasome.compound: S-GLSP (mixed: 42 compounds — flavonoids, alkaloids, terpenoids, saccharides)
NLRP3_inflammasome.finding: S-GLSP INHIBITS NLRP3 in AD model — opposite directionality from PMID 40450627. Spore powder (broken-shell, lipid+terpene-rich) ≠ EPS (β-glucan). Suggests cell-wall vs spore-extract pharmacology diverges — important for compound-level Phase 5 design.

NLRP3_inflammasome.title: [Ganoderma lucidum extract] Modulates Inflammatory Responses following 1-Methyl-4-Phenyl-1,2,3,6-Tetrahydropyridine (MPTP) Administration in Mice.
NLRP3_inflammasome.finding: GLE represses microglial NLRP3 in Parkinson model. Confirms G. lucidum extracts inhibit NLRP3 in CNS contexts.

NLRP3_inflammasome.title: Ganoderma lucidum polysaccharide alleviates cognitive dysfunction by inhibiting neuroinflammation via NLRP3/NF-κB signaling pathway.
NLRP3_inflammasome.finding: GLP inhibits NLRP3 in cognitive-dysfunction model. Note: this β-glucan INHIBITS NLRP3 here, contradicting PMID 40450627's EPS finding — context-dependent. Phase 5 must distinguish polysaccharide structures.

NLRP3_inflammasome.title: Anti-Inflammatory and Hepatoprotective Effects of Ganoderma lucidum Polysaccharides against Carbon Tetrachloride-Induced Liver Injury in Kunming Mice.
NLRP3_inflammasome.finding: GLPS suppresses NLRP3/ASC/caspase-1 in CCl4 liver injury.

NLRP3_inflammasome.title: Structural basis of immunomodulation by edible fungal polysaccharides: From molecular characteristics to action mechanisms.
NLRP3_inflammasome.finding: Review: β-glucan triple-helix conformation activates macrophage receptors. NLRP3 engagement is structure-dependent (MW, branching, sulfation). Supports our directionality-caveat finding above.

NLRP3_inflammasome.title: Fungal Polysaccharides as Modulators of Molecular Pathways in Liver Health.
NLRP3_inflammasome.finding: Review of fungal polysaccharide regulation of NF-κB / Nrf2 / NLRP3 in liver disease — confirms multi-species hepatic NLRP3 modulation.

NLRP3_inflammasome.title: Gut microbiota dysbiosis in hyperuricaemia promotes renal injury through the activation of NLRP3 inflammasome.
NLRP3_inflammasome.finding: NLRP3 axis confirmed as load-bearing in HUA-driven renal injury — establishes the chokepoint relevance for fungal compound program.

NLRP3_inflammasome.title: Celastrol ameliorates Propionibacterium acnes/LPS-induced liver damage and MSU-induced gouty arthritis via inhibiting K63 deubiquitination of NLRP3.
NLRP3_inflammasome.finding: MSU-induced gouty arthritis NLRP3 mechanism comparator (non-fungal) — reference for our gout NLRP3 framing.

C5aR1_complement.title: Complement and periodontitis.
C5aR1_complement.finding: Establishes C5aR1 subversion as immune-evasion mechanism; not a fungal antagonist.

C5aR1_complement.title: Complement and inflammasome crosstalk in chronic inflammation.
C5aR1_complement.finding: Crucial mechanism reference: C3a/C5a/MAC directly prime NLRP3, inflammasome-derived IL-1β/IL-18 amplifies complement — confirms §1.21 platform-gap rationale (C5aR1 inhibition would synergize with NLRP3 inhibition). NO fungal antagonist surfaced.

C5aR1_complement.title: Effects of beta-glucans on the immune system.
C5aR1_complement.finding: DIRECTIONALITY: mushroom β-glucans ACTIVATE complement (via CR3/iC3b opsonization) — opposite of inhibition. Same pattern as NLRP3 directionality issue: fungal cell-wall β-glucans are immune ACTIVATORS, not antagonists. C5aR1 antagonist program will need synthetic-route compounds, not whole-mushroom polysaccharides.

Hyperuricemia_gout_general.title: Hypouricemic Effect of Submerged Culture of [Ganoderma] in Potassium Oxonate-Induced Hyperuricemic Rats.
Hyperuricemia_gout_general.finding: GM at 200-400 mg/kg lowers serum UA from 5.56 to 4.45 mg/dL, downregulates hepatic XOD activity. GP improves renal function (BUN, ALT). Direct anti-hyperuricemia evidence for G. lucidum culture products.

Hyperuricemia_gout_general.title: [Ganoderma lucidum] polysaccharide peptide alleviates hyperuricemia by regulating adenosine deaminase and urate transporters.
Hyperuricemia_gout_general.finding: GLPP reduces blood UA up to 40.6% in HUA mice via ADA inhibition + GLUT9 down + OAT1 up. Hits THREE chokepoints: ADA, GLUT9, OAT1. Adenosine deaminase as a fungal-druggable target is novel for our chokepoint map.

Hyperuricemia_gout_general.title: Antihyperuricemic and Renal Protective Effect of Cordyceps chanhua (Ascomycetes) Fruiting Bodies in Acute Hyperuricemia and Chronic Gout Rodent Models.
Hyperuricemia_gout_general.evidence_level: animal (acute HUA mice + chronic gout rats)
Hyperuricemia_gout_general.finding: FBCC reduces SUA + suppresses XOD + reduces renal tubulointerstitial damage in chronic gout rats. Strong chronic-gout evidence.

Hyperuricemia_gout_general.title: Amelioration of hyperuricemia by cordycepin and Cordyceps militaris aqueous extract in mice via modulating gut microbiota and restoring metabolic profile.
Hyperuricemia_gout_general.finding: CME inhibits XO + reduces purine precursor availability + modulates gut microbiota. Cordycepin's anti-HUA effect comparable to whole CME.

Hyperuricemia_gout_general.title: Clerodendranthus spicatus-Cordyceps cicadae regulates mitophagy and protects renal tubular epithelial cells from hyperuricemic nephropathy.
Hyperuricemia_gout_general.finding: CS-CC restores PINK1-mediated mitophagy + reduces renal fibrosis in HUA nephropathy. Mitophagy as a NEW chokepoint — not in our current Open Enzyme map.

Hyperuricemia_gout_general.title: Cordyceps sinensis (a traditional Chinese medicine) for kidney transplant recipients.
Hyperuricemia_gout_general.evidence_level: CLINICAL — Cochrane systematic review of RCTs
Hyperuricemia_gout_general.finding: CLINICAL EVIDENCE: 5 RCTs (n=447) showed Cordyceps reduces hyperuricemia, hyperlipidemia, hyperglycemia, liver injury vs azathioprine in transplant patients. Quality unclear but clinical-grade evidence exists.

Hyperuricemia_gout_general.title: Efficacy of Cordyceps sinensis as an adjunctive treatment in kidney transplant patients: A systematic-review and meta-analysis.
Hyperuricemia_gout_general.finding: CLINICAL: 9 RCTs (mostly Chinese, including CNKI sources). Cordyceps + cyclosporine A reduces hyperuricemia and CNI nephrotoxicity. Concrete clinical evidence for hyperuricemia indication.

Hyperuricemia_gout_general.title: Hypouricemic and nephroprotective roles of anthocyanins in hyperuricemic mice.

PPAR_gamma.title: Biotransformation-derived metabolites from Astragalus membranaceus and Cordyceps militaris alleviate hyperuricemia via multi-target regulation.
PPAR_gamma.finding: Activates hepatic PPARα signaling pathway alongside URAT1/GLUT9/ABCG2 effects. Same paper as URAT1 entry — multi-chokepoint extract.

ergothioneine_thioredoxin_TXNIP._note: Search returned 2234 hits but top-relevance hits were dominated by oxidative-stress mechanism reviews, not fungal-source primary studies. Phase 5 should query LOTUS/ChEMBL directly for ergothioneine + thioredoxin chokepoint binding rather than relying on PubMed for this axis.

Ganoderma_lucidum.indication: NLRP3 ACTIVATION (anti-cryptococcal — wrong direction for gout)

Inonotus_obliquus_chaga.indication: anticancer focus, immune modulation (no direct gout/HUA hit found)

Trametes_versicolor_PSK._note: Search returned only 2 hits, neither directly addressing NLRP3 or gout chokepoints in our target frame. Phase 5 may need direct ChEMBL query for PSK / coriolan structural derivatives.

Pleurotus_ostreatus._note: Search returned 189 hits dominated by general nutrition/cholesterol papers. Lovastatin/cholesterol-axis only — not direct chokepoint relevance for our XO/URAT1/NLRP3/C5aR1 frame.

high_signal_findings: DUAL-CHOKEPOINT HIT: Ganoderma applanatum 2,4-DAE (PMID 35750011) inhibits BOTH XOD and URAT1 in mouse model — structurally allopurinol-like, in vivo SUA reduction from 407 to 134 µmol/L. This is the strongest single-compound finding from the breadth scan.

CORDYCEPIN AS DIRECT URAT1 MODULATOR: Cordyceps militaris cordycepin (PMID 29422889) — dose-response in mice, SUA 337→203 µmol/L, mechanism proven via ELISA/RT-PCR/WB. Already a well-characterized molecule (3'-deoxyadenosine) with established pharmacology.

CLINICAL-LEVEL EVIDENCE: Cordyceps sinensis has TWO meta-analyses (PMIDs 26457607 Cochrane + 28137532) of RCTs in transplant patients showing hyperuricemia reduction. Highest evidence-tier finding in the entire scan — clinical, not preclinical.

MULTI-CHOKEPOINT EXTRACT: AMC-BFE (Cordyceps militaris × Astragalus biotransformation, PMID 41905012, 2026) hits URAT1 + GLUT9 + ABCG2 + PPARα simultaneously. Bidirectional fermentation enriches isoflavone aglycones — koji-relevant biotransformation strategy comparator.

ABCG2 + NLRP3 SIMULTANEOUS HIT: Poria cocos in Fuling-Zexie formula (PMID 37788785) modulates ABCG2/GLUT9/OAT1 + suppresses NLRP3/ASC/cleaved-caspase-1. Poria is the only fungal ingredient — needs decomposition to identify which compound class drives which axis.

POLYSACCHARIDE DIRECTIONALITY PROBLEM: G. lucidum exopolysaccharides ACTIVATE NLRP3 in cryptococcosis (PMID 40450627, beneficial) but G. lucidum spore powder INHIBITS NLRP3 in Alzheimer (PMID 41378217). β-Glucan structure (MW, branching, sulfation, triple-helix conformation) determines directionality (PMID 40592243 review). Critical for Phase 5: spore powder ≠ EPS ≠ fruiting-body β-glucan. Cell-wall vs cytoplasmic vs lipid-fraction extracts have OPPOSITE NLRP3 effects.

C5aR1 PLATFORM GAP CONFIRMED (per validation-experiments.md §1.21): NO direct fungal antagonist of C5aR1 surfaced in PubMed scan. β-glucans ACTIVATE complement (CR3/iC3b opsonization, PMID 17895634) — opposite of inhibition. Complement-NLRP3 crosstalk review (PMID 41983141) confirms the mechanistic synergy rationale but identifies no natural fungal small-molecule antagonist. Phase 5 C5aR1 work will need synthetic-derivative or non-fungal-source approaches; whole-mushroom polysaccharide programs will not fill this gap.

NOVEL CHOKEPOINT — MITOPHAGY: Cordyceps cicadae regulates PINK1-mediated mitophagy in HUA nephropathy (PMID 40334761). Not in current Open Enzyme chokepoint map — worth surfacing to validation-experiments.md as a new candidate.

NOVEL CHOKEPOINT — ADENOSINE DEAMINASE (ADA): Ganoderma lucidum GLPP polysaccharide-peptide inhibits ADA (PMID 36385640). ADA upstream of XO — earlier intervention point. Worth adding to chokepoint map.
Sanghuangporus vaninii davallialactone (PMID 36801789): XO IC50 = 90 µM. Fruiting-body XO inhibitor at characterized purity (97.7%). Mid-tier potency (allopurinol IC50 ~7 µM) but novel scaffold.

MULTILINGUAL CATCH-NET: PubMed indexed extensive Chinese-author primary research (Shandong U, Peking U, Northwest A&F, Shanghai Academy, Guangdong CM University). The TCM × hyperuricemia search (PMID 38907332, 39176260, etc.) confirms the 'Chinese-language clinical evidence is real' framing — do not gate on Western evidence alone.

Lp-PLA2 (15 hits total) and HDAC6 (155 hits total): essentially zero direct fungal hits on these chokepoints. Phase 5 will need ChEMBL/LOTUS querying or structural prediction work — PubMed primary literature is empty.

Trametes versicolor / PSK: only 2 hits total, neither NLRP3-focused. PSK is well-characterized as immune adjuvant in Japan, but PubMed primary research on PSK × NLRP3 is essentially absent. Phase 5 may need direct screening.

Pleurotus ostreatus / oyster mushroom: 189 hits dominated by lovastatin/cholesterol. Statin axis is real (Pleurotus produces lovastatin natively) but does not map to our XO/URAT1/NLRP3/C5aR1 chokepoints. Off-target for current scope.
phase_5_recommendations: Top 3 compounds for Phase 5 deep-dive: (1) Ganoderma applanatum 2,4-DAE (dual XO+URAT1, PMID 35750011); (2) Cordyceps militaris cordycepin (URAT1 + already characterized as 3'-deoxyadenosine, PMID 29422889 + clinical track via Cordyceps sinensis); (3) Sanghuangporus vaninii davallialactone (novel XO scaffold, PMID 36801789).
Multi-chokepoint extract for whole-organism modeling: AMC-BFE Cordyceps × Astragalus biotransformation (PMID 41905012) — shows what fungal solid-state fermentation can produce.

Polysaccharide work needs structural specification: triple-helix β-glucan vs sulfated β-glucan vs spore-derived terpenoid mix produce OPPOSITE NLRP3 effects. Phase 5 must specify polysaccharide structure before claiming inflammasome modulation.
Add ADA and PINK1/mitophagy to Open Enzyme chokepoint map (validation-experiments.md) — both surfaced as fungal-druggable in this scan but absent from current map.
C5aR1 platform-gap: scan confirms PubMed primary fungal literature has no antagonist. Either accept gap or pivot to chemical synthesis route or non-fungal source for that chokepoint.
